LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 301, AS TO THE JAN-28-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CERTIFIED BLUE RECYLING, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AGAINST BLUEWATER ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC. (DOE 1); THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE UNOPPOSED MOTION IS GRANTED AND CERTIFIED BLUE RECYCLING, INC.'S DEFAULT IS SET ASIDE. THIS DEFENDANT SHALL ANSWER OR OTHERWISE RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT WITHIN 20 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. METROPOLITAN IS ORDERED TO SHOW CAUSE WHY IT SHOULD NOT BE SANCTIONED IN THE AMOUNT OF $250 FOR ITS FAILURE TO FOLLOW SFLR 2.7B. THE OSC WILL BE SET FOR HEARING ON FEBRUARY 25, 2025, AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 301 OF THIS COURT (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

Plaintiff Metropolitan Electrical Construction took the defaults of two non-appearing defendants, Bayview Environmental Services and Bluewater Environmental Services. Those defendants later moved to set aside the defaults. Metropolitan opposed Bayview's motion and did not oppose Bluewater's motion. None of those three parties served courtesy copies of their papers in advance of the hearing, in contravention of San Francisco Local Rule 2.7B.

The hearing was originally set for December 27, 2024. The court continued the matter on its own motion to January 28, 2025. The court's minute order stated, "All parties to provide courtesy copies per SF Local Rule 2.7B with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date."

Before the January 28, 2025 hearing, Metropolitan did not comply with the court's December 27, 2024 order to provide courtesy copies. The court issued an order to Metropolitan to show cause why it should not be sanctioned $250 for its failure to follow the local rule. Metropolitan now opposes sanctions, arguing that the rule itself does not include sanctions as a consequence.

Metropolitan is correct. The rule does not; it is the violation of this court's December 27 minute order that provides the basis for the sanction. (See CCP 177.5 ["A judicial officer shall have the power to impose reasonable money sanctions . . . payable to the court, for any violation of a lawful court order by a person, done without good cause or substantial justification."].) The court's order to show cause was insufficiently precise and should have specified that it was the violation of the December 27 minute order ordering compliance with Rule 2.7B that provided the contemplated basis for sanctions. The court discharges the OSC without sanction, but with a reminder to Metropolitan to adhere carefully to the court's local rules and any court orders.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2025 Continued To May-20-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Paul A. Herp; Declaration Of Richard G. Cleveland; [Proposed] Order (Motions)
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT BAYVIEW ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Set Aside Default.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The opposed motion is granted. "It is the policy of the law to favor, wherever possible, a hearing on the merits . . . ." (Murray & Murray v. Raissi Real Estate Development, LLC (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 379, 388 [citing Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 854].) Here, Bayview has presented evidence to rebut the presumption of proper service established by Evidence Code 647, leaving it open for this court to weigh the evidence of service presented by the parties. In any event, relief is proper under CCP 473(b). Bayview has acted diligently since learning of the default and Metropolitan presents no evidence of prejudice. Bayview's default is set aside. This defendant shall answer or otherwise respond to the complaint within 20 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for Metropolitan failed to provide courtesy copies of filed briefs as required by SFLR 2.7B. This matter was previously on calendar on December 27, 2024 but was continued for failure to provide courtesy copies. Metropolitan is ordered to show cause why it should not be sanctioned in the amount of $250 for its failure to follow SFLR 2.7B. The OSC will be set for hearing on February 25, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 301 of this court.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
